summary: End-to-end testing of new TSP algorithm features (held-karp / backtracking / greedy + /api/compare). Backend: 11 new pytest cases (TestAlgorithmsMeta, TestOptimizePerAlgorithm, TestCompare) added in /app/backend/tests/test_algorithms_compare.py — all 11 pass; combined regression of 22 backend tests (5 geocode tests skip due to outbound network restriction) all green. Frontend: Verified all 4 buttons (run-held-karp-btn, run-backtracking-btn, run-greedy-btn, compare-algorithms-btn) render with correct labels & complexity badges; all disabled when locations<2 then enabled after Manhattan-8 preset. Sequential runs: Greedy Σ=2240.78, Held-Karp Σ=2206.30, Backtracking Σ=2206.30 (greedy >= optimal ✓; held-karp == backtracking ✓). Compare modal opens, shows 3 cards with complexity/total cost/elapsed/optimality/space/engine/tour order, CHEAPEST badge on Held-Karp, FASTEST badge on Greedy, HEAD-TO-HEAD MATRIX table present, footer explains exact vs heuristic. Modal closes via X button AND via outside click. All required data-testids present.

minor.issue: Backtracking is reported as is_optimal=false with gap_pct=0.003% on tiny inputs (e.g. SAMPLE_5 euclidean → held-karp 46.67 vs backtracking 46.6712). Root cause: the C++ Held-Karp binary outputs cost with limited (≈2-decimal) precision, so the Python-computed backtracking cost is FP-greater than the C++ cost even though both are exact. Compare endpoint then flags backtracking sub-optimal and the Compare modal shows '+0.003%' instead of 'OPTIMAL'. Fix: in /api/compare, compare via abs(a-b) < epsilon (e.g. max(1e-3, optimum*1e-5)) instead of strict 1e-6, OR have the C++ binary emit cost with %.6f. Confirmed on Manhattan-8 the issue does not show up because cost magnitude is small.

integration_issues.issue: Backtracking card sometimes shows '+0.00%' (yellow) instead of green 'OPTIMAL' badge because backend gap_pct is 0.003% > 0 (see backend minor issue). Cosmetic but misleading — backtracking IS exact.

issues: Status bar shows static 'ALGO HELD-KARP · ENGINE C++' regardless of which algorithm was just run (greedy/backtracking still display 'HELD-KARP'). Consider making it reflect activeAlgo.

action_items: (MINOR/COSMETIC) Tighten /api/compare optimum tolerance OR increase C++ binary cost output precision so backtracking is correctly flagged is_optimal=true on small inputs.
(MINOR) Wire bottom-status-bar's ALGO label to activeAlgo state (currently hardcoded HELD-KARP).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 386: gap_pct uses strict optimum comparison; tiny FP differences from C++ binary cause exact backtracking to be flagged sub-optimal. Use a tolerance.

server.py: solve_backtracking uses recursive DFS without iterative limit — for n=15 worst case factorial explosion; the existing branch-and-bound prune helps but a hard wall-clock timeout would be safer (currently no timeout at all on backtracking unlike C++ subprocess which has timeout=30).

server.py: solve_held_karp_via_cpp parses cost with float() but the C++ binary apparently truncates to 2 decimals — see issue above. Either way, segments are recomputed in Python so segment sum may differ from cost field by ~0.001.
App.js still ~1700+ lines — split into smaller modules (carry-over from iter2/3).
logger reference order issue from iter3 still not addressed (logger created near bottom of server.py at line 514 but used earlier at lines 313 and 455).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_algorithms_compare.py (new, 11 tests covering algorithms metadata, per-algorithm optimize, compare endpoint validation + correctness)

success_rate.backend: 100% (22/22 active; 5 geocode skipped due to network)

seed_data_creation: MongoDB optimizations history populated organically by algo runs during testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 algorithms work correctly end-to-end (held-karp via C++, backtracking + greedy in Python). Compare modal shows the required CHEAPEST/FASTEST badges + head-to-head matrix and closes both via X and outside-click. Only outstanding nit is the tiny FP gap between C++ Held-Karp cost (truncated precision) and Python backtracking cost — this causes backtracking to display '+0.00%' instead of 'OPTIMAL' on some euclidean inputs. Fix is one-liner in /api/compare. The 13+ location cap on /api/compare is enforced by Pydantic max_length=12 (returns 422). Frontend cap shows toast 'Compare is capped at 12 nodes' (verified by code inspection in App.js but not reproduced in browser due to data setup overhead).
rca of the issue: C++ Held-Karp binary appears to output cost rounded/truncated (≈2-decimal). When /api/compare picks the minimum of held-karp vs backtracking as `optimum`, held-karp wins by a fraction (e.g. 46.67 < 46.6712). The is_optimal check then uses abs(cost - optimum) < 1e-6 which fails for backtracking. Mitigation: use a relative tolerance (e.g. < 1e-4 of optimum) when computing is_optimal; or fix the C++ binary to print cost with %.6f.
